matched_terms: dtn; schur; boundary; interface; bright; dark; complement; handoff
Name: Tool-interface principle
Metaphor: The CNNA tool locally exposes an active phase as a bright object against the complementary rest of the Lean codebase and its still unaudited semantic surroundings.
Mapping: Bright sector = active phase/current object; dark complement = remaining Lean code, open dependencies, unreviewed theory links, and unaudited semantics; boundary/interface = Master-YAML, status, object register, proof dossiers, module/import DAG and later database views; DtN/Schur-like reduction = extraction of relevant dependencies, modules, handoffs and review duties; noise/uncertainty = semantic errors, missing blue markings, incomplete expert review or generated-artifact defects; Birdsview = database/DAG/review view over the whole formal project, still secondary.
Operational consequence: Each active phase must make its boundary to the remaining project explicit: dependencies, module interfaces, review duties, blue/reference markings and hidden complement risks must be visible before local closure is trusted.
Boundary: This is an architecture metaphor for documentation and review practice, not a formal CNNA theorem, not an ontic claim, and not a replacement for Lean-primary verification or expert review.

Title: GeneralizedDtN/MultiSchur
Consumer direction: No special cross-phase consumer contract recorded yet. If a later phase consumes this output, record export shape and import direction before implementation.
Regression protocol: Not applicable unless phase_type is refactor or the phase changes imports/declarations. If refactor applies: wrapper-first, bounded migration package, local full-build evidence before closeout.
Lean/mathlib policy: Baseline is Lean 4.28.0/mathlib snapshot unless an explicit phase-boundary upgrade gate is opened. New imports must be verified against the chosen baseline; noncomputable/classical pressure must be logged and isolated.
Concrete traversal: Infrastructure-only or not prepared yet. Before operational code implementation, name a concrete finite instance and the path from input to output.
Mathlib complication log: No phase-specific mathlib complication recorded yet.
Noncomputable boundary audit: No phase-specific noncomputable-boundary audit recorded yet.

Title: Horizontal/vertical Schur-complement duality dependency-order lock
Consumer direction: One-way only: earlier route phases export S05/O05/TM0/CD7/BR06/BR07 to BR08; BR08/CD8 may not backfeed into their definitions.
Regression protocol: Not applicable unless phase_type is refactor or the phase changes imports/declarations. If refactor applies: wrapper-first, bounded migration package, local full-build evidence before closeout.
Lean/mathlib policy: Baseline is Lean 4.28.0/mathlib snapshot unless an explicit phase-boundary upgrade gate is opened. New imports must be verified against the chosen baseline; noncomputable/classical pressure must be logged and isolated.
Concrete traversal: Infrastructure-only gate; it requires later S07/S08/BR06/BR07 phases to show concrete finite reference/variation/full-cut instances before operational use.
Mathlib complication log: No new mathlib import in this planning gate.
Noncomputable boundary audit: No Lean boundary touched; later route phases must audit noncomputable/classical pressure if they import additional mathlib APIs.

Title: Implementation module manifest and semantic placement gate
Reusable existing modules: CNNA/PillarA/Arithmetic/Notation.lean as source to split, not as target monolith; existing Smoke/Test generator modules as candidates for generator-like work
Manifest rule: Closed contract: every scratchpad record must expose proposed_added_modules, proposed_modified_modules, reusable_existing_modules, semantic placement, rejection/fit decision, public/legacy/API role, import direction, expected downstream consumers, phase_type obligations, refactor regression protocol, version impact, concrete-instance traversal, generic test placement, mathlib complication log, and noncomputable boundary audit before Lean edits.
Semantic placement: The manifest is planning infrastructure and belongs to the single YAML master plus generated views. It is not a Lean module and must not be implemented as phase-local code. Future code modules are created only when an existing semantic module cannot be safely extended or wrapped.
Reuse/duplication audit: Phase 1.1.1.1.1 names reusable candidates but defers semantic generator classification to 1.1.1.1.2. Duplicate implementation remains blocked already at manifest level.
Canonical generator policy: Any generator-like phase must consume this manifest, then pass through 1.1.1.1.2 before Lean code. The manifest table must state whether O07/canonical smoke-test generator is reused, extended, or rejected.
Compactness rule: Closed rule: phase granularity is not file granularity. A later phase must justify every new module by semantic ownership, API/firewall direction, or unavoidable import split.
Duplicate risks: new generator/request/output helper names without canonical generator route; IdealThread.carrier/family singleton facts; CutSpec.full carrier facts; RecursiveSchurSource/MobiusTransfer recurrence logic; string-like analogy used as generator
Consumer direction: Every proposed public wrapper must state its expected consumers and import direction. BoundaryOperatorProfile must prepare for Phase 4.1 without importing Phase 4. For BR08/CD8 specifically, earlier route modules export data downstream and must not import the bridge.
Regression protocol: This gate does not refactor code, but it must require every later refactor manifest to declare wrapper-first/incremental vs atomic mode. Default for 1.1.2 is wrapper-first incremental with local full-build evidence per package.
Lean/mathlib policy: Every module manifest must state whether it stays on Lean 4.28.0/mathlib baseline, uses no mathlib change, or requires a separate upgrade gate.
Concrete traversal: This active gate is infrastructure-only: it will not introduce operational Lean code. For every later operational phase, the manifest must include a concrete finite instance path from generated input to intended endpoint. This phase must plan generic finite test traversal, not only special sentinels, for every upcoming computable path.
Mathlib complication log: No new mathlib import in this gate. Later manifests must list expected mathlib imports and whether each is executable, noncomputable-only, classical-only, or mirror-layer-only. Scalar architecture check added: any proposed new module must classify Complex/Real/QuadraticAlgebra dependencies under LEG31. Derived-quadratic update: any proposed QuadraticAlgebra/AdjoinRoot/ExactQuadratic module must state whether it is a schema, D-instantiation, proof/mirror reference, or executable coordinate layer.
Noncomputable boundary audit: No Lean operational code changed. Later phases must document noncomputable leakage risk before implementation.

Title: Required exact quadratic scalar schema: discriminant-indexed minimal operational interface
Added modules: none at decision gate; possible CNNA/PillarA/Foundation/ExactQuadraticComplex.lean only after manifest
Semantic placement: scalar-foundation / bridge governance, not generator semantics
Reuse/duplication audit: must check existing ExecComplex, ExecComplexBridge, Schur.CharpolyC, ArithmeticCDBridge, and mathlib QuadraticAlgebra before proposing new module
Canonical generator policy: scalar layer must consume canonical generator outputs only; cannot define generator semantics
Compactness rule: do not create ExactQuadraticComplex unless existing foundation/bridge modules cannot carry the semantics cleanly
Duplicate risks: alternate complex scalar, algebraic complex, CD complex, mirror Complex, exact quadratic norm/trace/conjugation
Consumer direction: scalar layer consumes generator/arithmetic source data; generator definitions do not consume scalar comparison/mirror data
Regression protocol: incremental wrapper mode; full local build green after each migration step
Lean/mathlib policy: 4.28.0 baseline unless version gate explicitly opens upgrade
Concrete traversal: Schema-level witnesses: symbolic arbitrary D, plus later planned special rows D=-1/-3/-4 only after D-ledger. This phase may include hand-written closed coordinate examples but cannot claim CNNA provenance for D.
Mathlib complication log: Audit QuadraticAlgebra coordinate operations, basis/noncomputable declarations, and version stability. AdjoinRoot quotient construction includes noncomputable instances in docs and must not silently enter operational path.
Noncomputable boundary audit: No analytic sqrt, Real, Complex exponential, elliptic functions, or uninspected quotient field structure in operational code.

Title: Computable scalar architecture decision: ExecComplex boundary, required exact quadratic layer, and Cayley-Dickson noncircularity lock
Added modules: none at decision gate; possible CNNA/PillarA/Foundation/ExactQuadraticComplex.lean only after manifest
Semantic placement: scalar-foundation / bridge governance, not generator semantics
Reuse/duplication audit: must check existing ExecComplex, ExecComplexBridge, Schur.CharpolyC, ArithmeticCDBridge, and mathlib QuadraticAlgebra before proposing new module
Canonical generator policy: scalar layer must consume canonical generator outputs only; cannot define generator semantics
Compactness rule: do not create ExactQuadraticComplex unless existing foundation/bridge modules cannot carry the semantics cleanly
Duplicate risks: alternate complex scalar, algebraic complex, CD complex, mirror Complex, exact quadratic norm/trace/conjugation
Consumer direction: scalar layer consumes generator/arithmetic source data; generator definitions do not consume scalar comparison/mirror data
Regression protocol: no code movement in this gate
Lean/mathlib policy: 4.28.0 baseline unless version gate explicitly opens upgrade
Concrete traversal: required later: QxQ baseline row, Q(i) row, Q(sqrt(-3)) row, and at least one generic D row if O09 is implemented
Mathlib complication log: Record Complex/Real noncomputable boundary, QuadraticAlgebra instance diamond warning, NumberField formal/non-executable dependency, and any required version-gate.
Noncomputable boundary audit: General C/R and algebraic closure remain mirror/formal. Exact quadratic candidate must avoid noncomputable definitions on operational path.
